title: Link-Bearing Elements, Relations, Base URLs, and Crawl Graph · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds semantic provenance-rich link graph from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of semantic provenance-rich link graph while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate URL-bearing HTML elements.
Use first valid base semantics against final response URL and retain accepted and rejected base evidence.
Interpret rel tokens, canonical, alternate, hreflang, download, media, nofollow, and target under explicit owner policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Elements
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 8820 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify link elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and provenance-rich graph edges; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Elements.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate url-bearing html elements. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Link Elements, keep Link Elements connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
